back_translation: And I saw, in the right hand of the One seated on the throne, a scroll-book that was written on the inside and on the back, sealed with seven seals
And I saw a mighty angel proclaiming with a loud voice, ‘Who is worthy to open this scroll-book and to break those seals?’
And no one in heaven, on earth, or under the earth was able to open that scroll-book or to look in it
And I wept very greatly, because no one was worthy to open that scroll-book or to look in it
And one of the elders said to me, ‘Do not weep. Behold, the lion from the tribe of Judah, the root of David, has overcome to open this scroll-book and its seven seals’

And I saw, in the midst of the throne and of the four living-creatures, and in the midst of the elders, a Lamb standing, as if slaughtered. He had seven horns and seven eyes, which are the seven Spirits of God that are sent out throughout all the earth
And He came and took that scroll-book from the right hand of the One seated on the throne

And when He took that scroll-book, the four living-creatures and the twenty-four elders fell down before the Lamb. Each one held a harp and a golden bowl that was full of incense, which are the prayers of the saints

And they sang a new song, ‘You are worthy to receive this scroll-book and to open those seals, because You were slaughtered, and You redeemed people for God by Your blood, from every tribe, every tongue, every people, and every nation
And You have made them to be a kingdom and priests for our God, and they will reign on the earth’
And I looked, and heard the voice of many angels around the throne, and the living-creatures, and the elders. And the number of them was a myriad of myriads, and a thousand of thousands
They said with a loud voice, ‘The Lamb who was slaughtered is worthy to receive mighty-power, riches, wisdom, strength, honor, glory, and praise’

And every creature that is in heaven, on earth, under the earth, in the sea, and all things that are in them, I heard saying, ‘To the One seated on the throne, and to the Lamb, may praise, honor, glory, and dominion be to Him forever and ever’
And the four living-creatures said, ‘Amen.’ And the elders fell down and worshiped
